title: Finding the Value of Each Digit in 4,728,351

steps.instruction: Write each digit and its place name from left to right.
steps.work: 4 = millions, 7 = hundred-thousands, 2 = ten-thousands, 8 = thousands, 3 = hundreds, 5 = tens, 1 = ones
steps.explanation: A 7-digit number has places from millions down to ones.

steps.instruction: Multiply each digit by its place value.
steps.explanation: Each place is 10 times the value of the place to its right.
The sum matches the original number, confirming our place values are correct.

steps.instruction: Find every 6 in the number 6,606,066 and name its place.
steps.work: 6 in millions, 6 in ten-thousands, 6 in tens, 6 in ones... Wait, let me recount: 6,606,066 → 6(millions), 6(hundred-thousands), 0(ten-thousands), 6(thousands), 0(hundreds), 6(tens), 6(ones).
steps.explanation: Be careful to identify each digit's place. It helps to write one digit at a time.
The same digit (6) has different values depending on its place.

steps.instruction: How many times greater is the millions 6 than the ones 6?
steps.explanation: The 6 in the millions place is one million times greater than the 6 in the ones place.
